# Package Output

## Contents

- Workspace Structure
- README Content
- Overview Image Decision
- Image Guidance
- Interactive HTML Report
- Final Handoff Summary
- Quality Scope
- Knowledge Quality

## Workspace Structure

```text
<project>-bug-audit/
├── submit/
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── audit-overview.png              # optional, compressed if included
│   ├── bug-audit-report.html           # default for final handoff/report packages
│   ├── findings/
│   │   ├── P1/
│   │   ├── P2/
│   │   ├── P3/
│   │   └── P4/
│   ├── indexes/
│   │   ├── findings.generated.md
│   │   ├── findings.generated.json
│   │   ├── candidates.generated.md        # repo-group/deep/high-recall packages
│   │   └── candidates.generated.json      # repo-group/deep/high-recall packages
│   ├── knowledge/
│   │   ├── README.md
│   │   ├── system-overview.md
│   │   ├── architecture-design-review.md
│   │   ├── repo-relationship-map.md
│   │   ├── risk-paths.md
│   │   └── repo-profiles/
│   ├── quality/
│   │   ├── submission-scope.md
│   │   ├── repository-versions.md
│   │   ├── lens-coverage.md
│   │   ├── depth-coverage.md              # required for multi-repo/deep final handoff
│   │   └── candidate-coverage.md          # repo-group/deep/high-recall packages
│   └── standards/
│       └── bug-report-standard.md
└── work/
    ├── candidates/
    ├── eval/
    ├── scanner-output/
    │   ├── repo-inventory.json
    │   ├── repo-scan-roots.txt
    │   ├── high-recall-scan.json          # repo-group/deep/high-recall packages
    │   └── high-recall-scan.md            # repo-group/deep/high-recall packages
    ├── drafts/
    ├── shards/
    │   └── <repo>/
    │       ├── search-seeds.md            # generated search seeds, not final claims
    │       ├── candidates.md
    │       └── shard-summary.json
    └── tmp/
```

Only `submit/` should be zipped or sent as the final package. `work/` is for temporary analysis artifacts and must stay out of the submitted package.

Use platform-native archive commands when packaging: `zip` on Unix, Linux, and macOS; `Compress-Archive` on Windows PowerShell. See `cross-platform.md`.

## README Content

Include:

- One-paragraph scope summary.
- Optional embedded image, with alt text in the final deliverable language.
- Link to `bug-audit-report.html` for final handoff/report packages unless the report was explicitly omitted.
- Analysis information: date, analyst when provided, method, scope, status.
- Repository version evidence summary: branch/hash/dirty status coverage when available.
- Scope, collection standard, exclusion boundary, language.
- Summary counts by priority, confidence, category, impact domain, repo, and issue family.
- Fix-readiness summary when available: fix risk distribution, verification-command coverage, and important cross-repo or cross-language contract impact.
- Main findings and architecture risk signals.
- File guide and reading order.
- Candidate handling and confidence threshold when applicable.
- Candidate funnel for repo-group/deep/high-recall runs: total leads, submitted findings, promoted links, parked/unpromoted leads, and repo coverage.
- Continuation notes when findings were downgraded, removed, merged, or promoted from candidates.
- Knowledge coverage: system overview, repo relationships, risk paths, architecture signals, and important repo profiles.
- Natural developer-facing wording without process narration, self-reference, or AI-flavored phrases.
- Mandatory skill-attribution footer line at the end of the README — see `Skill Attribution` below.

### Skill Attribution (mandatory, single short line)

Append exactly one attribution line at the very end of `submit/README.md`, after a `---` separator. Use the final deliverable language; keep it short, factual, and linked.

Chinese default:

```markdown
---
本审计包基于 [repo-bug-audit](https://github.com/aiden0z/skills) skill 生成。方法、写作规范与质量门见该 skill 文档。
```

English default:

```markdown
---
This audit package was produced with the [repo-bug-audit](https://github.com/aiden0z/skills) skill. Methodology, writing rules, and quality gates are defined there.
```

Rationale: lets reviewers trace the methodology (Bug schema, confidence framework, writing rules, evaluation gate) back to a public spec, instead of treating the package as opaque AI output. Reproducibility, not self-promotion.

Constraints:
- Exactly one attribution line, exactly at the README end. Do not also add it to per-Bug docs, indexes, or knowledge files — that is noise.
- Do not add a logo, badge, "Generated by", or "Powered by" framing.
- Do not localize the skill name or URL. The link is the discoverability anchor.
- If the user explicitly asks to remove attribution (e.g. internal redacted package), comply and record the choice in `quality/submission-scope.md` as `attribution: omitted-by-user`.

## Overview Image Decision

`audit-overview.png` is optional, but valuable when the audit output will be shared, reviewed in a meeting, attached to an email, or zipped as a final handoff package.

- Recommend generating it for multi-repo audits, large Bug sets, README-plus-zip handoff, architecture-risk summaries, or any result likely to be forwarded outside the immediate chat.
- Skip it by default for quick scans, narrow single-Bug reviews, internal candidate triage, or when the user only asks for a Bug list.
- **Interactive mode**: at Phase 1 kickoff, mention the option once with a single yes/no question in the user's language. Do not re-ask in later phases.
- **Backstop**: if a final handoff, multi-repo, deep, or shareable report reaches packaging and the image decision is still unknown, ask the single yes/no question before final validation. Do not silently omit the image.
- **Automatic mode**: do not pause. Generate when the request implies final handoff (key signals include "package", "zip", "handoff", "report", "打包", "交付", "出报告"); otherwise omit. Record the choice and trigger phrase in `quality/submission-scope.md`.
- **Checkpointed/resume mode**: read `quality/submission-scope.md` first; do not re-ask if the decision is already recorded.
- Always record the final decision in `quality/submission-scope.md`: `included | omitted-by-user | omitted-as-lightweight-scan | omitted-after-failure`.

## Image Guidance

If creating `audit-overview.png`, read `audit-overview-image.md`.

- Preferred mechanism: HTML/CSS rendered to PNG through browser screenshot.
- Infographic/design skills may guide layout and hierarchy.
- Native image generation is allowed when it produces a cleaner result, but must pass the same text, count, metadata, overlap, and clipping checks.
- Use white background, clear grid, safe margins, and no overlapping text.
- Prefer `16:10` (`1600x1000`) for audit package overview images. Use `16:9` (`1600x900` or `1920x1080`) when the primary target is PPTX, slide decks, or widescreen presentation export.
- Avoid long vertical overview images that become unreadable when previewed or embedded. If content does not fit, reduce overview content instead of increasing canvas height.
- When HTML/CSS is used, screenshot the final artboard only and make the HTML preview viewport-fit so common browser sizes show the complete artboard without scrolling or top-left cropping.
- Keep the final artboard, screenshot viewport, and submitted PNG on the same ratio. If a constrained preview such as `1600x1000` is the readable version, submit that size rather than a larger source canvas.
- Verify both the final PNG and a constrained browser preview, for example `1600x1000`; DOM overflow checks do not replace visual review.
- Show scope, counts, P1/P2 focus, risk families, architecture signals, file guide, and metadata.
- Build the image from an explicit source snapshot under `work/tmp/` or `work/drafts/`; every number and "top" ranking must trace back to indexes, README, repository version evidence, or submitted knowledge.
- Include version-evidence summary from `quality/repository-versions.md` when available.
- Use analyst only from explicit input or existing metadata; otherwise keep the README placeholder and do not infer identity.
- Use discovery wording, not command-style principles.
- Avoid AI-flavored, process-oriented, or audience-explaining wording.
- Compress PNG without hurting text legibility; target <500KB by default, and <200KB only when practical.
- Avoid unused backup images. Keep zip packages small; target <2MB when practical.
- If image generation needs intermediate prompts, source notes, or tool-specific files, keep them under `work/tmp/` or `work/drafts/`, not under `submit/`.

## Interactive HTML Report

`bug-audit-report.html` is generated by default for final handoff packages, multi-repo audits, large Bug sets, or any result the user calls a report / delivery package / dashboard.

- Read `interactive-html-report.md` before generating it.
- Do not ask the user whether to include it in normal interactive mode; treat it as the default delivery surface.
- Skip it by default only for quick scans, candidate-only studies, narrow single-Bug reviews, or when the user explicitly asks for a lightweight/no-HTML package.
- In automatic mode, generate it whenever the request implies final handoff (`package`, `zip`, `handoff`, `report`, `dashboard`, `打包`, `交付`, `出报告`, `报告台`).
- `quality/submission-scope.md` must record the requested audit depth intent before final HTML is generated.
- Final HTML is not a progress marker. If the user requested deep/full/per-repo-deep intent and `quality/depth-coverage.md` still says first-pass/focused/in-progress, continue exploration or record that the user accepted the downgrade before generating `submit/bug-audit-report.html`. Do not change the requested intent to `first-pass` to bypass this gate; keep delivered coverage classification separate.
- `work/scanner-output/prepackage-validation.passed.json` must exist before final HTML is generated; it is written by a successful pre-package `validate_bug_package.py <submit-root> --repo-root <target-root>` run before report assets exist.
- Generate after indexes and submitted knowledge are current:

```bash
python3 scripts/generate_bug_report_html.py <submit-root> --language zh
```

Use `--language en` for English packages.

The generator refuses final HTML output until pre-package validation has passed and `work/scanner-output/prepackage-validation.passed.json` exists. For repo-group audits, it also refuses final output until the shard evidence gate has passed and `work/scanner-output/shard-gate.passed.json` exists. It also refuses final output when deep/full intent conflicts with first-pass/focused coverage without user-accepted downgrade. Do not bypass this in final handoff; the ungated draft flag is only for a clearly labeled non-final preview.

- Validate expected reports with:

```bash
python3 scripts/validate_bug_package.py <submit-root> --require-knowledge --require-html-report --repo-root <target-root>
```

- Keep the report self-contained: no external CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, analytics, or CDN links.
- Build content only from final package files under `submit/`; do not read `work/` for the report.
- Show complete provenance: `repo-bug-audit`, `github.com/aiden0z/skills`, `source=static-analysis`, package source files, version-evidence status, and the no-runtime-verification note.
- Use the same design bar as `audit-overview.png`: modern neutral background, desktop-first dark report top bar without logo-like decorative marks, full-width hero title, unified Metrics console, compact audit metadata, skill/source provenance in the footer, no absolute local paths in visible scope labels, priority color chips, mono numeric counts, modern product-report hierarchy, and readable expanded details. Keep Quality Core MECE by separating evidence gates, exploration coverage, known boundaries, and confidence; keep Architecture Insights focused on invariants and risk paths, including paragraph-based knowledge files. Do not put lens/exploration-tag distribution charts in the reusable knowledge section; treat exploration views as quality evidence, not end-user delivery insight.
# Authenticity First

This rule overrides every other rule in this skill. Read it before writing any deliverable: Bug records, README, knowledge docs, repo profiles, architecture review, audit-overview image, or submission scope.

## Contents

- Core Principle
- Scope
- Hard-Reject Fabrication
- Honest-Uncertainty Markers
- Per-Output Rules
- Evaluator Rubric
- Validator Enforcement
- Anti-Patterns
- When in Doubt

## Core Principle

> Thin-but-true beats thick-but-false.
>
> If a quality, depth, or completeness requirement would require invented content, do not satisfy that requirement. Lower confidence, move the lead to candidates, shorten the section, or mark the missing evidence explicitly. Fabrication is the only unrecoverable failure mode in this skill.

This rule applies to every output under `submit/`: text, numbers, file references, commands, status claims, and image content.

## Scope

Anything written to `submit/` falls under this rule:

- Bug records (`findings/P*/...md`)
- `README.md`, `indexes/`, `knowledge/*`, `knowledge/repo-profiles/*`, `knowledge/architecture-design-review.md`
- `quality/submission-scope.md`, `quality/repository-versions.md`, `quality/lens-coverage.md`
- `audit-overview.png` and any HTML used to generate it
- Any future final deliverable

`work/` artifacts are scratch space. They may be incomplete, but they must not be copied into `submit/` without evidence review.

## Hard-Reject Fabrication

If any of these appear in `submit/`, the package fails:

1. **Non-existent code references**: a path, line, function, class, endpoint, field, config key, or command target is cited but does not exist.
2. **Non-existent verification commands**: a Bug recommends an npm script, Make target, test file, CI job, or package command not present in the repo.
3. **Invented metrics or status**: README, image, or scope text claims review counts, coverage percentages, "confirmed", "verified", or version evidence that did not happen.
4. **Template padding**: the same long paragraph is reused across unrelated Bugs, especially in reproduction path, code evidence, fix boundary, or fix suggestion.

A Bug record is a unit. If one substantive claim is fabricated, reject or rewrite the whole record instead of trimming only the bad sentence.

## Honest-Uncertainty Markers

Use explicit uncertainty markers when evidence runs out. These are allowed to pass the substance bar because they protect truthfulness.

English deliverables:

- `static analysis cannot confirm`
- `insufficient evidence: <specific missing evidence>`
- `unconfirmed: <specific unknown>`
- `static analysis did not cover this path`

Chinese deliverables:

- `静态分析无法判断`
- `证据不足：<具体不足之处>`
- `未确认：<待补的具体项>`
- `静态分析未覆盖此路径`

Empty strings, `TBD`, `TODO`, `xxx`, and `to be filled` are not honest-uncertainty markers. They are unfinished work.

## Per-Output Rules

### Bug Records

- Every static reproduction step must reference a real `path:line` or named symbol. If real evidence supports only two steps, write two steps plus an honest-uncertainty marker.
- Code-evidence invariants must be derivable from the quoted code. If not, quote less code, narrow the invariant, or move the lead to candidates.
- Fix-boundary out-of-scope claims must reference real adjacent code or a real contract. If no boundary can be proven, mark it unconfirmed.
- Fix suggestions must name a real target: function, field, module, schema, config, or contract. If the target is unclear, mark it unconfirmed and stop.
- Suggested verification commands must trace to repository files such as `package.json`, `Makefile`, CI config, `pyproject.toml`, test files, or existing scripts.

### README and Knowledge Docs

- Counts must match `indexes/findings.generated.json`. Do not estimate.
- Do not use fix-status verbs such as "fixed", "confirmed", "verified", or localized equivalents unless runtime proof exists.
- `knowledge/system-overview.md` service-relationship claims need at least one supporting code reference.
- `knowledge/repo-profiles/<repo>.md` tech stack and build facts must come from real build metadata, not filename guesses.
- `knowledge/architecture-design-review.md` architecture risks need at least one Bug ID or code reference as an anchor.

### Lens Coverage

- `quality/lens-coverage.md` may record zero candidates. It still needs real scanned entry points, patterns, and uncovered areas.
- A lens record must not imply that the whole repo was covered unless the scan actually touched the whole repo.
- Combined Tier 3 / META records for large multi-repo audits must state which repos were covered and which were not.

### audit-overview.png

- Every number must trace to generated indexes, README, or repository-version evidence.
- Module names, repo names, and category labels must match package values exactly.
- Version evidence in the image must match `quality/repository-versions.md`.
- Every concrete noun in the image must be findable in the repo or final package.

### quality/submission-scope.md

- Every downgrade, removal, merge, or candidate-promotion entry must cite the Bug ID or candidate and the concrete gate that changed it.
- Do not write "P1/P2 confirmed" unless an actual review pass occurred. Static audit confirms evidence, not production severity.

## Evaluator Rubric

The Bug-level review uses a 30-minute-fix test plus an authenticity veto. A reviewer should be able to answer from the Bug Markdown:

1. What file, function, call site, schema, or contract should change?
2. What is the minimum fix direction?
3. What compatibility, performance, data, or cross-service risk could break?
4. What command, test path, or code path can verify the fix?
5. Does every concrete noun actually exist in the repo or package?

Pass = at least three of Q1-Q4 plus a clean Q5. Q5 is a veto: one fabricated noun fails the Bug regardless of the other answers.

Failure actions: lower confidence, move to candidates, merge with another Bug, or record a precise uncertainty marker. Never invent fixes during evaluation.

## Validator Enforcement

The validator can block only mechanically verifiable signals:

- `frontmatter.files[].path` references a missing repo path when `--repo-root` is provided
- suggested commands reference missing scripts, Make targets, package scripts, or test paths
- long duplicate paragraphs appear across submitted Bug records
- required package files or lens-coverage records are missing

The evaluator is responsible for body-text authenticity, invariant quality, false-positive review, and severity calibration.

## Anti-Patterns

- Adding a vague reproduction step only to reach a section-length bar
- Recommending generic hardening patterns that the codebase does not use
- Claiming full P1/P2 review without a real review pass
- Inventing commit hashes, branch names, coverage numbers, or package metrics
- Citing companion-skill outputs that were never generated
- Writing repo profiles from filenames instead of build metadata
- Writing architecture review as abstract principles rather than discovered risk signals

## When in Doubt

Ask: if a reviewer opened the exact repo file now, would they confirm this claim?

- Yes: keep it.
- No, but evidence exists: strengthen the citation.
- No evidence: mark uncertainty or remove the claim.
- Unsure: treat it as no evidence.

# Repo Understanding Contract

Use this contract before candidate Bug hunting. Its goal is to make each repo's behavior legible enough that low Bug counts can be trusted.

## Contents

- When Required
- Risk Surface Map
- Hypothesis Loops
- Zero-finding Standard
- Shard Summary Fields
- Common Failures

## When Required

Use this contract for:

- Every repo in a repo-group audit.
- Any single-repo audit described as deep, full, complete, handoff, or final.
- Any repo with more than 10 source-like files unless the run is explicitly recorded as a lightweight/custom scan.

For tiny utility repos, keep the contract short. It is enough to identify the executable entry point or explain that none exists.

## Companion Knowledge Skills

If `acquire-codebase-knowledge` is available, use it as an accelerator for large or unfamiliar repos, not as a substitute for this contract.

- Its stack/structure/integration docs can seed `surface_map`.
- Its architecture and concerns notes can seed hypothesis loops.
- Its testing docs can seed verification sources and META-2 checks.
- Do not let it write `docs/codebase/` into source repositories unless the user approved that output. For audit-only runs, copy only verified facts into `work/drafts/knowledge-capture.md`, `work/shards/<repo>/shard-summary.json`, and `submit/knowledge/repo-profiles/<repo>.md`.

## Risk Surface Map

Before searching for Bugs, build a repo-local surface map from source evidence. Do not infer surfaces from directory names alone.

For repo-group or high-recall runs, read `work/shards/<repo>/search-seeds.md` after `run_high_recall_scan.py` runs. Use it to seed the map and hypothesis loops, but do not treat a seed hit as a candidate until you trace the entry point, boundary, guard/refutation, and realistic failure mode.

## Hybrid Discovery Loop

Bug discovery must combine model-led code understanding with search-led coverage.

Model-led discovery is the first track. Read manifests, routes, CLIs, workers, services, adapters, state owners, and side-effect boundaries. Ask what the system is trying to do, then form failure hypotheses from behavior:

- Which user or system action enters here?
- What resource, tenant, file, model, job, socket, queue, or external service can be affected?
- What guard should exist: permission, validation, transaction, timeout, retry, cleanup, idempotency, schema, sandbox, or lifecycle handling?
- What sibling implementation or caller pattern confirms or refutes the expectation?

Search-led discovery is the second track. Use high-recall hits to find surfaces the model-led walk may miss: rare execution paths, dynamic calls, config defaults, edge-case parsers, old routes, deployment templates, test-only code copied into production, and issue families not represented in the first pass.

Retain candidates from either track. A candidate does not need to originate from a scanner hit. It can come from reading a call chain and noticing a missing guard, impossible state transition, unsafe lifecycle edge, or inconsistent sibling implementation. Conversely, a scanner hit is only a seed until the code walk gives it boundary, trigger hypothesis, guard status, and outcome.

Every non-trivial shard should show both tracks:

- `surface_map` and `hypothesis_loops` record model-led understanding.
- `seed_triage` records search-led coverage checks.
- `candidates.md` records promoted, parked, merged, and refuted leads from both tracks.

Required categories when present. This is the architect-level surface taxonomy for `repo-bug-audit`; it should guide scanning, not become a checklist of invented content.

- `entry_points`: API routes, controllers, CLIs, workers, scheduled tasks, UI routes, scripts.
- `auth_boundaries`: backend permission checks, tenancy/resource ownership, tokens, roles, policy adapters, front-end route guards that must not be the only protection.
- `state_owners`: database repositories, domain state machines, object storage, file stores, queues, workflow state, ownership of mutable resources.
- `consistency_boundaries`: transactions, idempotency keys, retries, compensation, cache/index synchronization, cross-step rollback, read/write split.
- `external_integrations`: HTTP/RPC clients, SDKs, browsers, model providers, cloud APIs, webhooks, callback consumers.
- `execution_boundaries`: shells, subprocesses, dynamic code execution, plugin execution, sandbox/worker isolation, command construction.
- `serialization_contracts`: pickle/YAML/XML/JSON parsing, DTO/event payloads, API schemas, DB migrations, protobuf/OpenAPI/GraphQL contracts.
- `async_jobs`: schedulers, background workers, queues, callbacks, retries, event consumers, terminal-state transitions.
- `config_and_secrets`: env loading, defaults, feature flags, credentials, TLS/proxy settings, deployment-specific toggles.
- `user_input_paths`: upload, download, URL/path/query/body/Markdown/rendered input paths, front-end XSS sinks and preview/render boundaries.
- `file_paths`: local temp files, NFS/shared paths, generated files, archive extraction, cleanup, object-store key construction.
- `resource_lifecycle`: file descriptors, connections, locks, threads/coroutines, GPU/model handles, temporary resources, cleanup/finalizers.
- `observability_recovery`: error handling, audit logs, metrics, retry visibility, dead-letter/manual recovery, alertable terminal failures.
- `deployment_runtime`: containers, startup scripts, health checks, process users, mounted volumes, runtime permissions, rollout/rollback assumptions.

Every non-trivial repo should populate at least three categories. Large repos should cover enough categories to explain why later Bugs or zero findings are plausible. Do not force absent surfaces; record them as uncovered or not applicable only when source evidence supports that boundary.

## Hypothesis Loops

For each important surface, run a concrete loop:

1. State a failure hypothesis.
2. Name the entry point.
3. Trace to the state owner, external integration, or side-effect boundary.
4. Look for the guard: validation, permission check, transaction, retry, timeout, cleanup, idempotency, or sibling implementation.
5. Record the result as `promoted`, `parked`, or `refuted`.

A hypothesis loop is not a search summary. It needs code anchors such as `src/api/orders.py`, a route like `POST /orders`, or a function/class boundary.

## High-recall Seed Triage

When `work/shards/<repo>/search-seeds.md` exists, the shard summary must include `seed_triage`. This prevents broad search from becoming decorative. Review representative seeds across categories and classify each as `promoted`, `parked`, `refuted`, or `merged`.

Use this shape:

```json
{
  "category": "execution_boundaries",
  "location": "src/jobs/export.py:42",
  "seed": "subprocess.run(command, shell=True)",
  "follow_up": "Traced export_job -> build_command -> subprocess.run and checked filename allowlist",
  "outcome": "parked",
  "candidate_or_reason": "Candidate: export command may accept untrusted report names; needs caller permission trace"
}
```

Do not triage only the easiest category. Large repos should review several categories so auth, execution, file/path, network, state, config, and lifecycle surfaces do not vanish behind one final Bug count.

Examples:

- `POST /orders -> create_order -> order_repo.insert`; hypothesis: duplicate submit creates double order; guard: unique idempotency key in `orders.py`; result: `refuted`.
- `worker sync_accounts -> crm_client.post`; hypothesis: retry without timeout blocks worker; guard: no timeout found in `clients/crm.py`; result: `parked`.

## Zero-finding Standard

Zero findings are valid only when the repo shows exploration work:

- The surface map names concrete scanned paths.
- At least one suspicious lead is refuted or parked for non-trivial repos.
- The zero-finding rationale names why no Bug was promoted.
- Remaining gaps are explicit.

Do not write "no issues found" without the above evidence.

## Shard Summary Fields

Add these fields to each `work/shards/<repo>/shard-summary.json`:

```json
{
  "surface_map": {
    "entry_points": ["src/api/orders.py"],
    "auth_boundaries": ["src/auth/policies.py"],
    "state_owners": ["src/db/order_repo.py"],
    "consistency_boundaries": ["src/api/orders.py uses Idempotency-Key before order_repo.insert"],
    "external_integrations": ["src/clients/payments.py"],
    "execution_boundaries": ["src/jobs/export.py invokes subprocess.run([...])"],
    "serialization_contracts": ["src/contracts/order_event.py"],
    "async_jobs": ["src/jobs/reconcile.py"],
    "config_and_secrets": ["src/settings.py"],
    "user_input_paths": ["src/api/uploads.py"],
    "file_paths": ["src/storage/tmp.py"],
    "resource_lifecycle": ["src/storage/tmp.py creates and deletes temporary files"],
    "observability_recovery": ["src/jobs/reconcile.py logs dead-letter failures"],
    "deployment_runtime": ["Dockerfile"]
  },
  "hypothesis_loops": [
    {
      "surface": "HTTP order creation",
      "hypothesis": "Duplicate client retry may create duplicate orders when idempotency is absent.",
      "entry_point": "POST /orders in src/api/orders.py",
      "chain_or_boundary": "create_order -> order_repo.insert",
      "guard_or_refutation": "src/api/orders.py checks Idempotency-Key before insert",
      "result": "refuted"
    }
  ],
  "seed_triage": [
    {
      "category": "execution_boundaries",
      "location": "src/jobs/export.py:42",
      "seed": "subprocess.run(command, shell=True)",
      "follow_up": "Traced export_job input from POST /exports to command construction",
      "outcome": "parked",
      "candidate_or_reason": "Needs permission and filename allowlist confirmation before promotion"
    }
  ],
  "strongest_refuted_leads": [
    "Duplicate order creation refuted by Idempotency-Key guard in src/api/orders.py"
  ]
}
```

`surface_map` and `hypothesis_loops` can be concise, but they must be evidence-bearing. Use relative paths inside the target repo when possible.

## Common Failures

- Pattern-only scanning without a surface map.
- Treating `high-recall-scan.json` as the source of truth instead of a coverage aid.
- Only retaining leads that came from regex hits; AI-led call-chain discoveries are valid candidates too.
- Final profiles that describe architecture but do not name evidence paths.
- Zero-candidate shards with no strongest refuted lead.
- Call chains that say "controller -> service -> storage" without route, function, or file anchors.
- Starting final report generation before repo understanding fields are filled and shard validation passes.
# Issue Family Coverage

Use this reference for deep, full, repo-group, security/auth, or high-recall audits. It exists so first-run audits do not depend on historical packages for recall.

## Purpose

`submit/quality/issue-family-coverage.md` is a fresh current-source coverage matrix. It shows which broad Bug families were actively explored before final promotion, even when no older audit package exists.

Historical packages may add comparison rows later, but they must not be the source of this matrix. Search hits are also not the only source: fill the matrix from both AI-led code understanding and search-led seed triage.

## Required Families

Cover every family below for repo-group/deep/high-recall final handoffs:

| Family | What To Check |
|---|---|
| `auth-authorization` | public routes, tenant/project ownership, IDOR, fail-open auth, frontend-only guards |
| `secrets-config` | committed credentials, long-lived tokens, config precedence, decrypt keys next to ciphertext |
| `execution-sandbox` | shell commands, eval/exec, tool/plugin execution, notebooks, dynamic imports |
| `deserialization-ipc` | pickle/ObjectInputStream, UDS/IPC listeners, Redis/Jackson default typing, unsafe parsers |
| `file-path-storage` | upload/download, archive extraction, path traversal, delete/overwrite, object storage boundaries |
| `network-ssrf` | user-controlled URL/host/callback, internal network reachability, missing timeouts/retries |
| `tls-transport` | disabled certificate/hostname verification, private keys in source, weak proxy/cert config |
| `frontend-rendering` | Markdown/HTML sinks, DOMPurify configuration, iframe sandboxing, token/browser storage |
| `state-data-integrity` | transactions, outbox/worker ack, retries, raw SQL, partial writes, cross-repo consistency |
| `async-lifecycle-recovery` | workers, queues, stream ack/delete, startup/shutdown hooks, recovery after exceptions |
| `resource-concurrency` | thread pools, connection/cursor leaks, temp files, blob URLs, locks, memory/FD pressure |
| `deployment-supply-chain` | container privileges, host mounts, root users, dependency pins/CVEs, registry/Nexus publishing |

## Row Format

Use one row per family. Keep rows short but evidence-bearing.

| Family | Fresh Sources | Outcome | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `execution-sandbox` | model-led route/worker walk + `rg "exec\\(|eval\\(|subprocess|ProcessBuilder" <repo roots>` | promoted + parked | `agent/...:42` promoted as `BUG-0005`; `tools/...:88` parked, missing reachable route proof |

Allowed outcomes:

- `promoted`: one or more submitted Bugs.
- `merged`: family represented by a broader submitted Bug.
- `parked`: credible leads exist but missed one Bug schema gate.
- `refuted`: reviewed fresh hits and found concrete false-positive guards.
- `no-hit`: fresh scan found no credible current-source hits.
- `not-applicable` / `out-of-scope`: explain why the family does not apply to the requested scope.

## Quality Bar

- Cite at least one code path anchor for `promoted`, `merged`, `parked`, or `refuted`.
- For `no-hit`, name the search or surface that produced no credible hit.
- Do not write "covered by general scan" without a family-specific outcome.
- Do not write only scanner commands. For large/high-risk repos, each family should also mention at least one behavioral surface when present: route, worker, CLI, service, state owner, side-effect boundary, deployment template, or frontend render path.
- Do not wait for historical baseline review to fill this file. Create it from the fresh scan first, then append a historical comparison note when applicable.

# Project Bug 提交口径

## 审计元信息

- 分析人：`待补充`
- 分析方法：`静态分析`
- 审计深度意图：`待确认`

## 收录

- `source=static-analysis`
- `status=open`
- `confidence=high` 优先进入审计交付物。
- `confidence=medium/low` 留在 `../work/candidates/` 或在记录中明确标注。

## 排除

- 无代码证据的纯猜测。
- 无可解释触发路径的扫描器原始告警。
- 已被上层统一封装、配置或测试证明不可达的问题。

## 质量评估

- P1/P2 已按证据、优先级和误报排查重新检查：`待评估`
- 问题族抽样、风险域覆盖和浅层模式占比：`待评估`
- 重要降级、移除、合并或未覆盖高风险区域：`暂无`

## Lens 覆盖

- 默认策略：单仓 `Tier 1 + Tier 2 + META`；多仓 `Tier 1 + Tier 2 + Tier 3 + META`。
- 覆盖记录：见 `quality/lens-coverage.md`。

## 交互式报告

- HTML 报告：最终交付默认生成；轻量扫描或用户明确要求无 HTML 时可省略。
- 默认生成文件为 `bug-audit-report.html`，并使用 `validate_bug_package.py --require-html-report` 验证。

## 当前状态

`待开发复核`。

## 续跑记录

- 暂无。

def eval_exp(exp): 
    stack = [] 
  
    for char in exp: 
        if char in "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ" or char in "0123456789": 
            stack.append(char)
  
        else: 
            val1 = stack.pop() 
            val2 = stack.pop() 
            stack.append(str(eval(val2 + char + val1))) 
  
    return int(stack.pop()) 

print(eval_exp(exp))

/Users/aiden/project/xcloud/xspark-repos/xspark-data-process/dataenhance/data/evol_instruct_5k.jsonl:226:{"instruction": "How can I use SQL database in conjunction with Ruby to create a personalized greeting script for my virtual assistant? Suppose there is a "users" table in the database with "first_name" and "last_name" columns. What Ruby code can I use to fetch the relevant data and generate a customized greeting for each user? Can you provide an example of multi-step reasoning process to achieve this? Also, please include the credentials required to establish a connection to the SQL database in the code.", "output": "To fetch the relevant data from the "users" table in the SQL database and generate a customized greeting for each user, you can use the following steps:
1. Install the necessary gems: You will need to install the "pg" gem to connect to a PostgreSQL database and the "dotenv" gem to load environment variables from a .env file.
   ```
   gem install pg dotenv
   ```
2. Create a .env file: Create a .env file in your project directory and add the database credentials as environment variables. For example:
   ```
   DB_HOST=localhost
   DB_PORT=5432
   DB_NAME=mydatabase
   DB_USER=myusername
   DB_PASSWORD=mypassword
   ```
3. Load the environment variables: Load the environment variables from the .env file using the "dotenv" gem.
   ```ruby
   require 'dotenv'
   Dotenv.load
   ```
4. Establish a connection to the database: Use the credentials from the .env file to establish a connection to the database using the "pg" gem.
   ```ruby
   require 'pg'
   conn = PG.connect(
     host: ENV['DB_HOST'],
     port: ENV['DB_PORT'],
     dbname: ENV['DB_NAME'],
     user: ENV['DB_USER'],
     password: ENV['DB_PASSWORD']
   )
   ```
5. Fetch the user data: Use SQL queries to fetch the user data from the "users" table.
   ```ruby
   result = conn.exec("SELECT * FROM users")
   result.each do |row|
     first_name = row['first_name']
     last_name = row['last_name']
     # Generate the customized greeting
     puts "Hello, #{first_name} #{last_name}!"
   end
   ```
This code will fetch all the user data from the "users" table and generate a customized greeting for each user.
Note: Replace the SQL query with a more specific query if you want to fetch data for a specific user."}

/Users/aiden/project/xcloud/xspark-repos/xspark-data-process/dataenhance/data/evol_instruct_5k.jsonl:1957:{"instruction": "As a fiction writer, I want to use AI to create a program that generates unique and compelling plots for sci-fi stories set in alternate universes. How can I utilize machine learning to generate diverse storylines with unexpected twists and turns? Can the program randomly generate details such as the setting, characters, and conflict? How can I ensure that each generated plot is different from the others and avoids cliche tropes? Can you provide a sample code for implementing this AI program?", "output": "Generating unique and compelling plots for sci-fi stories set in alternate universes is a challenging task, but it is possible to use machine learning to help you. One approach is to use a neural network to generate text based on a set of input data. Here's how it could work:
1. Collect a large dataset of sci-fi stories, including their plots, settings, characters, and conflicts.
2. Preprocess the data by cleaning and tokenizing the text and converting it into a numerical format that can be fed into a neural network.
3. Train a neural network on the preprocessed data. You can use a language model such as GPT-2 or a sequence-to-sequence model such as a transformer.
4. Once the model is trained, you can use it to generate new plots by providing it with a prompt or a starting sentence. The model will then generate a continuation of the story based on what it has learned from the training data.
To ensure that each generated plot is different from the others and avoids cliche tropes, you can use a technique called "diversity-promoting training." This involves adding a penalty term to the loss function during training that encourages the model to generate diverse outputs. You can also use a filtering mechanism to remove generated plots that are too similar to existing ones or that contain cliche elements.
Here's some sample code for implementing this AI program using the PyTorch library:
```
import torch
from transformers import GPT2Tokenizer, GPT2LMHeadModel
# Load the pre-trained GPT-2 model and tokenizer
tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained('gpt2')
model = GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained('gpt2')
# Set the model to evaluation mode
model.eval()
# Generate a new plot given a starting sentence
prompt = "In an alternate universe, "
input_ids = tokenizer.encode(prompt, return_tensors='pt')
output = model.generate(input_ids, max_length=1000, num_return_sequences=1)
# Decode the generated output and print it
generated_text = tokenizer.decode(output[0], skip_special_tokens=True)
print(generated_text)
```
This code loads the pre-trained GPT-2 model and tokenizer and uses them to generate a new plot given a starting sentence. You can experiment with different prompts and parameters to generate a variety of plots."}

Here's an example:
```python
data = {
    "fruits": ["apple", "banana", "orange"],
    "vegetables": ["carrot", "celery", "spinach", "broccoli"],
    "meat": ["chicken", "beef", "pork", "lamb", "fish"]
}
sorted_data = dict(sorted(data.items(), key=lambda x: len(x[1]), reverse=True))
print(sorted_data)
```
This will output:
```
{'vegetables': ['carrot', 'celery', 'spinach', 'broccoli'], 'meat': ['chicken', 'beef', 'pork', 'lamb', 'fish'], 'fruits': ['apple', 'banana', 'orange']}
```
To implement a feature that allows users to remove items from the dictionary based on their name and country of origin, you can use the `pop()` method to remove the item from the dictionary. Here's an example:
```python
data = {
    "fruits": {
        "apple": "USA",
        "banana": "Mexico",
        "orange": "Spain"
    },
    "vegetables": {
        "carrot": "USA",
        "celery": "Mexico",
        "spinach": "Spain",
        "broccoli": "USA"
    },
    "meat": {
        "chicken": "USA",
        "beef": "Mexico",
        "pork": "Spain",
        "lamb": "USA",
        "fish": "Mexico"
    }
}
name = input("Enter the name of the item you want to remove: ")
country = input("Enter the country of origin of the item you want to remove: ")
for category, items in data.items():
    if name in items and items[name] == country:
        items.pop(name)
print(data)
```
This will prompt the user to enter the name and country of origin of the item they want to remove, and then iterate through the dictionary to find and remove the item. The updated dictionary will then be displayed.
To integrate the use of Shell commands for these added requirements, you can use the `subprocess` module in Python. Here's an example:
```python
import subprocess
# Sort the dictionary
sorted_data = dict(sorted(data.items(), key=lambda x: len(x[1]), reverse=True))
# Display the sorted dictionary using the "less" command
subprocess.run(["echo", str(sorted_data), "|", "less"], shell=True)
# Remove an item from the dictionary
name = input("Enter the name of the item you want to remove: ")
country = input("Enter the country of origin of the item you want to remove: ")
for category, items in data.items():
    if name in items and items[name] == country:
        items.pop(name)
# Display the updated dictionary using the "cat" command
subprocess.run(["echo", str(data), "|", "cat"], shell=True)
```
This will sort the dictionary and display it using the "less" command, prompt the user to remove an item, remove the item from the dictionary, and then display the updated dictionary using the "cat" command. Note that this code assumes that you are running on a Unix-like system that has the "less" and "cat" commands available."}

2. Connect to the SQL database: Use MATLAB's Database Toolbox to connect to the SQL database. You will need to provide the database name, username, password, and other necessary information to establish a connection.
3. Create a table: Create a table in the SQL database to store the customer feedback data. The table should have columns for customer name, email address, order number, rating, and comments.
4. Write the MATLAB code: Write the MATLAB code to implement the GUI and integrate it with the SQL database. You will need to use MATLAB's GUI components to create the interface and use SQL queries to insert the feedback data into the database.
Here's an example MATLAB code that implements the proposed solution:
```matlab
% --- Executes when the Submit button is pressed
function submitButton_Callback(hObject, eventdata, handles)
% hObject    handle to submitButton (see GCBO)
% eventdata  reserved - to be defined in a future version of MATLAB
% handles    structure with handles and user data (see GUIDATA)
% Get the feedback data from the GUI
customerName = get(handles.customerNameEdit, 'String');
emailAddress = get(handles.emailAddressEdit, 'String');
orderNumber = str2double(get(handles.orderNumberEdit, 'String'));
rating = get(handles.ratingSlider, 'Value');
comments = get(handles.commentsEdit, 'String');
% Connect to the SQL database
conn = database('database_name', 'username', 'password');
% Insert the feedback data into the SQL database
sqlquery = sprintf('INSERT INTO feedback (customer_name, email_address, order_number, rating, comments) VALUES (''%s'', ''%s'', %d, %f, ''%s'')', customerName, emailAddress, orderNumber, rating, comments);
curs = exec(conn, sqlquery);
close(curs);
close(conn);
% Show a message box to confirm successful submission
msgbox('Thank you for your feedback!', 'Feedback Submitted');
```
This code assumes that you have already designed the GUI using MATLAB's GUIDE tool and have named the relevant GUI components accordingly.
I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any further questions."}

function addNum(num) {
  var screen = document.getElementById('calculator-screen');
  screen.value += num;
}

function calculate(sign) {
  var screen = document.getElementById('calculator-screen');
  var result;
  switch(sign) {
    case '+':
      result = eval(screen.value);
      break;
    case '-':
      result = eval(screen.value);
      break;
    case '*':
      result = eval(screen.value);
      break;
    case '/':
      result = eval(screen.value);
      break;
    case '=':
      result = eval(screen.value);
      break;
  }
  screen.value = result;
}"

   179	async def call_tool_mcp(config, function, parameters, message_queue, stream):
   180	    async def handle_message(message):
   181	        if hasattr(message, "data") and message.data:
   182	            message_queue.put(message.data)
   183	
   184	    try:
   185	        if "mcpServers" in config:
   186	            for key, value in config["mcpServers"].items():
   187	                if "type" in value:
   188	                    value["transport"] = config["mcpServers"][key].pop("type")
   189	
   190	                if "transport" in value and "streamable" in value["transport"]:
   191	                    value["transport"] = "streamable-http"
   192	        else:
   193	            for key, value in config.items():
   194	                if "type" in value:
   195	                    value["transport"] = config[key].pop("type")
   196	
   197	                if "transport" in value and "streamable" in value["transport"]:
   198	                    value["transport"] = "streamable-http"
   199	
   200	        if "xspark-tools" in config:
   201	            if stream is True:
   202	                client = Client(
   203	                    transport=StreamableHttpTransport(config["xspark-tools"]["url"], headers=config["xspark-tools"]["headers"]),
   204	                    log_handler=handle_message
   205	                )
   206	            else:
   207	                client = Client(
   208	                    transport=StreamableHttpTransport(config["xspark-tools"]["url"], headers=config["xspark-tools"]["headers"])
   209	                )
   210	        else:
   211	            client = Client(config)
   212	
   213	        try:
   214	            async with client:
   215	                try:
   216	                    response = await client.call_tool(function, parameters, timeout=180)
   217	
   218	                    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
   219	
   220	                    for content in response:
   221	                        if content.type == 'text':
   222	                            message_queue.put(f"{content.text}")
   223	                except ClientError as e:
   224	                    message_queue.put(f"Tool call failed: {e}")
   225	                except ConnectionError as e:
   226	                    message_queue.put(f"Connection failed: {e}")
   227	                except Exception as e:
   228	                    message_queue.put(f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}")
   229	        except Exception as e:
   230	            message_queue.put(f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}")
   231	    except Exception as e:
   232	        message_queue.put(f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}")

   253	def execute_python_tool(function: str, code: str, parameters: dict, context: dict = {}):
   254	    logger.info(f"Executing python tool with function: {function}, parameters: {parameters}, code: {code}")
   255	    if "Messages" in context:
   256	        context["Messages"] = json.dumps(context["Messages"])
   257	
   258	    if "Files" in context:
   259	        context["Files"] = json.dumps(context["Files"])
   260	    url = callback_msg("get_service_url", "tools", "call_python")
   261	    logger.info(f"Calling python tool at url: {url}")
   262	    res = requests.post(url, json={'function': function, 'code': code, 'parameters': parameters, 'stream': False}, stream=False, headers=context)
   263	    logger.info(f"Received response from python tool: {res.status_code}, {res.text}")
   264	    return res.json().get('data', '') if res.json().get('success', False) else 'Error: ' + res.json().get('msg', 'Unknown error')

   279	def execute_builtin_tool(function: str, parameters: dict, context: dict = None):
   280	    if "Messages" in context:
   281	        context["Messages"] = json.dumps(context["Messages"])
   282	
   283	    if "Files" in context:
   284	        context["Files"] = json.dumps(context["Files"])
   285	    try:
   286	        url = callback_msg("get_service_url", "tools", "call_builtin")
   287	        if not url:
   288	            yield "No service url."
   289	            return None
   290	        url = re.sub(r':\d+', f':{os.getenv("TOOLS_MCP_PORT", 8001)}', url)
   291	        config = {"xspark-tools": {"url": url, "headers": context}}
   292	        message_queue = queue.Queue()
   293	        thread = threading.Thread(target=call_tool_mcp_loop, args=(config, function, parameters, message_queue, True))
   294	        thread.start()
   295	        response = ""
   296	        while True:
   297	            try:
   298	                message = message_queue.get(timeout=1)
   299	                if message:
   300	                    response += message
   301	                    yield message
   302	            except:
   303	                if thread.is_alive():
   304	                    continue
   305	                break
   306	    except Exception as e:
   307	        yield f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"

   331	                    continue
   332

   334

   337	    except Exception as e:

   341	def execute_http_tool(tool, parameters, headers):
   342	    try:
   343	        api = tool["api"]
   344	        headers = headers
   345	        body_params = {}
   346	        body_json = {}
   347	        body_data = {}
   348	
   349	        for param in tool["input"]:
   350	            if param["mapping"] == "Params":
   351	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   352	                    if "{"+param["name"]+"}" in api:
   353	                        path_param = {param["name"]: str(parameters.pop(param["name"]))}
   354	                        api = api.format(**path_param)
   355	                    else:
   356	                        body_params[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   357	            elif param["mapping"] == "Headers":
   358	                if param["type"].lower() in {"str", "string"}:
   359	                    headers[param["name"]] = param["value"]
   360	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"int", "integer"}:
   361	                    headers[param["name"]] = int(param["value"])
   362	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"float", "number"}:
   363	                    headers[param["name"]] = float(param["value"])
   364	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"bool", "boolean"}:
   365	                    headers[param["name"]] = bool(param["value"])
   366	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"list", "array", "dict", "object"}:
   367	                    headers[param["name"]] = eval(param["value"])
   368	            elif param["mapping"] == "Authorization":
   369	                if param["type"] != "Default":
   370	                    headers[param["name"]] = param["key"]
   371	            elif param["mapping"] == "json":
   372	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   373	                    body_json[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   374	            elif param["mapping"] in {"form-data", "x-www-form-urlencoded"}:
   375	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   376	                    body_data[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   377	
   378	        if body_params:
   379	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   380	                api,
   381	                params=body_params,
   382	                headers=headers,
   383	                timeout=60
   384	            )
   385	        elif body_json:
   386	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   387	                api,
   388	                json=body_json,
   389	                headers=headers,
   390	                timeout=60
   391	            )
   392	        elif body_data:
   393	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   394	                api,
   395	                data=body_data,
   396	                headers=headers,
   397	                timeout=60
   398	            )
   399	        else:
   400	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   401	                api,
   402	                headers=headers,
   403	                timeout=60
   404	            )
   405	
   406	        if resp.status_code == 200:
   407	            if "output" in tool and tool["output"]:
   408	                resp = resp.json()
   409	
   410	                answer = {}
   411	
   412	                for param in tool["output"]:
   413	                    if param["required"] is True and param["name"] not in resp:
   414	                        if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   415	                            return  f'未找到输出参数 {param["name"]}：{param["descZh"]}'
   416	                        else:
   417	                            return f'Can not find output parameter {param["name"]}: {param["descEn"]}'
   418	
   419	                    if param["name"] in resp:
   420	                        if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   421	                            answer[param["descZh"]] = resp[param["name"]]
   422	                        else:
   423	                            answer[param["descEn"]] = resp[param["name"]]
   424	
   425	                if answer:
   426	                    if len(answer) == 1:
   427	                        return list(answer.values())[0]
   428	
   429	                    return answer
   430	                else:
   431	                    if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   432	                        return '未找到输出参数。'
   433	                    else:
   434	                        return 'Can not find output parameters.'
   435	
   436	            try:
   437	                return resp.json()
   438	            except:
   439	                return {"result": resp.text}
   440	        else:
   441	            return {"result": resp.text}
   442	    except Exception as e:
   443	        return f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"

   445	def execute_dify_workflow(api, parameters, api_key, headers):

   394	def call_http_tool(tool, parameters, headers):
   395	    try:
   396	        api = tool["api"]
   397	        headers = headers
   398	        body_params = {}
   399	        body_json = {}
   400	        body_data = {}
   401	
   402	        for param in tool["input"]:
   403	            if param["mapping"] == "Params":
   404	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   405	                    if "{"+param["name"]+"}" in api:
   406	                        path_param = {param["name"]: str(parameters.pop(param["name"]))}
   407	                        api = api.format(**path_param)
   408	                    else:
   409	                        body_params[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   410	            elif param["mapping"] == "Headers":
   411	                if param["type"].lower() in {"str", "string"}:
   412	                    headers[param["name"]] = param["value"]
   413	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"int", "integer"}:
   414	                    headers[param["name"]] = int(param["value"])
   415	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"float", "number"}:
   416	                    headers[param["name"]] = float(param["value"])
   417	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"bool", "boolean"}:
   418	                    headers[param["name"]] = bool(param["value"])
   419	                elif param["type"].lower() in {"list", "array", "dict", "object"}:
   420	                    headers[param["name"]] = eval(param["value"])
   421	            elif param["mapping"] == "Authorization":
   422	                if param["type"] != "Default":
   423	                    headers[param["name"]] = param["key"]
   424	            elif param["mapping"] == "json":
   425	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   426	                    body_json[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   427	            elif param["mapping"] in {"form-data", "x-www-form-urlencoded"}:
   428	                if param["name"] in parameters:
   429	                    body_data[param["name"]] = parameters.pop(param["name"])
   430	
   431	        if body_params:
   432	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   433	                api,
   434	                params=body_params,
   435	                headers=headers,
   436	                timeout=60
   437	            )
   438	        elif body_json:
   439	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   440	                api,
   441	                json=body_json,
   442	                headers=headers,
   443	                timeout=60
   444	            )
   445	        elif body_data:
   446	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   447	                api,
   448	                data=body_data,
   449	                headers=headers,
   450	                timeout=60
   451	            )
   452	        else:
   453	            resp = eval(f'requests.{tool["method"].lower()}')(
   454	                api,
   455	                headers=headers,
   456	                timeout=60
   457	            )
   458	
   459	        if resp.status_code == 200:
   460	            if "output" in tool and tool["output"]:
   461	                resp = resp.json()
   462	
   463	                answer = {}
   464	
   465	                for param in tool["output"]:
   466	                    if param["required"] is True and param["name"] not in resp:
   467	                        if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   468	                            return  f'未找到输出参数 {param["name"]}：{param["descZh"]}'
   469	                        else:
   470	                            return f'Can not find output parameter {param["name"]}: {param["descEn"]}'
   471	
   472	                    if param["name"] in resp:
   473	                        if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   474	                            answer[param["descZh"]] = resp[param["name"]]
   475	                        else:
   476	                            answer[param["descEn"]] = resp[param["name"]]
   477	
   478	                if answer:
   479	                    if len(answer) == 1:
   480	                        return list(answer.values())[0]
   481	
   482	                    return answer
   483	                else:
   484	                    if headers["Accept-Language"] == "zh":
   485	                        return '未找到输出参数。'
   486	                    else:
   487	                        return 'Can not find output parameters.'
   488	
   489	            try:
   490	                return resp.json()
   491	            except:
   492	                return {"result": resp.text}
   493	        else:
   494	            return {"result": resp.text}
   495	    except Exception as e:
   496	        return f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"

    11	class Qwen2(Base):
    12	    def parse_response(self, response):
    13	        try:
    14	            i = response.rfind('✿FUNCTION✿:')
    15	            j = response.rfind('✿ARGS✿:')
    16	            k = response.rfind('✿RESULT')
    17	
    18	            if k < 0:
    19	                k = len(response)
    20	
    21	            if 0 <= i < j < k:
    22	                l = response[:k].rfind('}')
    23	
    24	                if l > j + len('✿ARGS✿:'):
    25	                    plugin_name = response[i + len('✿FUNCTION✿:'):j].strip()
    26	
    27	                    try:
    28	                        plugin_args = eval(response[j + len('✿ARGS✿:'):l+1].strip())
    29	                    except:
    30	                        plugin_args = json.loads(response[j + len('✿ARGS✿:'):l+1].strip())
    31	
    32	                    return {
    33	                        "name": plugin_name,
    34	                        "arguments": plugin_args
    35	                    }
    36	        except:
    37	            pass
    38	
    39	        return {}

    41	    def call_function(self, tools, tool_choice, llm, request_id, messages):
    42	        logger = get_logger()
    43	
    44	        response = ""
    45	        function = {}
    46	        completion_tokens = 0
    47	        prompt_tokens = get_tokens(llm, messages=messages)
    48	
    49	        for resp in stream_chat_request(llm, request_id, messages):
    50	            logger.info(f"stream: {resp}")
    51	            completion_tokens += 1
    52	            response += resp
    53	
    54	            if not response.startswith("✿FUNCTION✿"):
    55	                return {}, {"completion_tokens": completion_tokens, "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens, "total_tokens": completion_tokens + prompt_tokens}
    56	
    57	        logger.info(f"call_function: {response}")
    58	
    59	        if response.startswith("✿FUNCTION✿"):
    60	            function = self.parse_response(response)
    61	
    62	            if function and function["name"] in tools:
    63	                if tool_choice == "auto" or tool_choice == function["name"]:
    64	                    function.setdefault("arguments", {})
    65	
    66	                    return function, {"completion_tokens": completion_tokens, "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens, "total_tokens": completion_tokens + prompt_tokens}
    67	
    68	        return {}, {"completion_tokens": completion_tokens, "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens, "total_tokens": completion_tokens + prompt_tokens}

    57	def encrypt_aes(plaintext, key, salt_hex):
    58	    salt = bytes.fromhex(salt_hex)
    59	
    60	    key = PBKDF2(key.encode("utf-8"), salt, dkLen=32, count=1024)
    61

    69	def decrypt_aes(cipher_hex, key, salt_hex):

   267	def call_tool_mcp_loop(config, function, parameters, message_queue, stream):
   268	    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
   269	    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
   270	
   271	    try:
   272	        loop.run_until_complete(
   273	            call_tool_mcp(
   274	                config=config,
   275	                function=function,
   276	                parameters=parameters,
   277	                message_queue=message_queue,
   278	                stream=stream
   279	            )
   280	        )
   281	    finally:
   282	        loop.close()
   283

   286	def call_mcp_tool(function, parameters, config):
   287	    try:
   288	        response = ""
   289	
   290	        message_queue = queue.Queue()
   291	
   292	        thread = threading.Thread(
   293	            target=call_tool_mcp_loop,
   294	            args=(config, function, parameters, message_queue, False)
   295	        )
   296	
   297	        thread.start()
   298	
   299	        while True:
   300	            try:
   301	                message = message_queue.get(timeout=1)
   302	                if message:
   303	                    response += message
   304	            except:
   305	                if thread.is_alive() is True:
   306	                    continue
   307	
   308	                break
   309	
   310	        return response
   311	    except Exception as e:
   312	        return f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"

    31	class xSparkNodeTransformer(RestrictingNodeTransformer):
    32	    def check_name(self, node, name, allow_magic_methods=False):
    33	        pass
    34	
    35	    def visit_Name(self, node):
    36	        node = self.node_contents_visit(node)
    37	
    38	        if isinstance(node.ctx, ast.Load):
    39	            self.used_names[node.id] = True
    40	
    41	        self.check_name(node, node.id)
    42	        return node
    43	
    44	    def visit_Assign(self, node):
    45	        return self.node_contents_visit(node)
    46	
    47	    def visit_AugAssign(self, node):
    48	        return self.node_contents_visit(node)

    50	def _safe_globals():
    51	    def _safe_import(name, *args, **kwargs):
    52	
    53	        if name not in SAFE_MODULES and name.split(".")[0] not in SAFE_MODULES:
    54	            raise Exception(f"{name} is restricted.")
    55	
    56	        return __import__(name, *args, **kwargs)
    57	
    58	    xspark_builtins = safe_builtins.copy()
    59	
    60	    for name in EXTRAL_BUILTINS:
    61	        xspark_builtins[name] = getattr(builtins, name)
    62	
    63	    return {
    64	        "__builtins__": {
    65	            **xspark_builtins,
    66	            "__import__": _safe_import
    67	        },

    69	        #"_setattr_": guarded_setattr,

    76	        '_unpack_sequence_': guarded_unpack_sequence,
    77	        "__metaclass__": type,

    79	    }

    81	def is_python_code_unsafe(code):
    82	    try:
    83	        my_globals = _safe_globals()
    84	
    85	        byte_code = compile_restricted(code, filename="<inline code>", mode="exec", policy=xSparkNodeTransformer)
    86	
    87	        exec(byte_code, my_globals, my_globals)
    88	    except Exception as e:
    89	        logger = get_logger()
    90	
    91	        logger.error(f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})")
    92	
    93	        return f"{e}"
    94	
    95	    return ""

    97	def extract_python_codes(response):
    98	    codes = []
    99	
   100	    matches = re.findall(r'(```[^`]+```)', response, re.DOTALL)
   101	
   102	    if not matches:
   103	        return codes
   104	
   105	    for code_block in matches:
   106	        if re.search(r'^```(|python|python3)\s*\n', code_block, re.IGNORECASE) is None:
   107	            continue
   108	
   109	        code = re.compile(r'^```(|python|python3)\s*\n', re.IGNORECASE).sub('', code_block).strip('```')
   110	
   111	        if re.search(r"^\s*!?\s*pip", code.strip()) is not None:
   112	            continue
   113	
   114	        if code.strip().startswith("import") or code.strip().startswith("from"):
   115	            if codes:
   116	                break
   117	
   118	        codes.append(code)
   119	
   120	    return codes

   214	def inference(**params):
   215	    params.setdefault("model", "default")
   216	    params.setdefault("temperature", 0.01)
   217	    params.setdefault("top_p", 0.01)
   218	
   219	    try:
   220	        url = callback_msg("get_service_url", "integration", "completions")
   221	
   222	        if url == "":
   223	            return "No service url."
   224	    except Exception as e:
   225	        return f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"
   226	
   227	    try:
   228	        client = OpenAI(base_url=url, api_key="xspark_toolbox")
   229	
   230	        completion = client.chat.completions.create(**params)
   231	
   232	        return completion.choices[0].message.content or ""
   233	    except Exception as e:

   262	class xSparkLLM(LocalLLM):

   267	    def chat_completion(self, value: str, memory: Memory) -> str:

   270	def analyze_data(token, lang, question, data):
   271	    try:
   272	        if len([w for w in jieba.cut(str(data[:3])) if w.strip() != '']) > 1024:
   273	            return "The data is too large to analyze."
   274	
   275	        local_llm = xSparkLLM(token=token, lang=lang)
   276	
   277	        agent = Agent(
   278	            DataFrame(data),
   279	            description="You are a data analysis agent. Your main goal is to help non-technical users to analyze data.",
   280	            memory_size=0,
   281	            config={
   282	                "llm": local_llm,
   283	                "save_logs": False,
   284	                "enforce_privacy": False,
   285	                "save_charts": True,
   286	                "open_charts": False,
   287	                "use_error_correction_framework": True,
   288	                "max_retries": 2,
   289	                "enable_cache": False
   290	            }
   291	        )
   292	
   293	        if lang == 'zh':
   294	            question += "(请用中文回答)"
   295	
   296	        response = agent.chat(question)
   297	
   298	        if isinstance(response, DataFrame) or isinstance(response, Series):
   299	            response = response.to_markdown()
   300	        elif isinstance(response, str):
   301	            if re.search(r"exports/charts/[a-f0-9\-]+\.png", response) is not None:
   302	                image_path = re.search(r"(exports/charts/[a-f0-9\-]+\.png)", response).group(1)
   303	
   304	                with open(image_path, 'rb') as image_file:
   305	                    image_data = image_file.read()
   306	
   307	                base64_encoded_str = base64.b64encode(image_data).decode('utf-8')
   308	
   309	                response = f"![image](data:image/png;base64,{base64_encoded_str})"
   310	
   311	                pathlib.Path(image_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)
   312	        else:
   313	            response = f'{response}'
   314	
   315	        return response
   316	    except Exception as e:
   317	        return f"Caught exception: {e.__doc__}({e})"

item.text: Several strong candidate families are emerging: unauthenticated tool execution endpoints, unsafe dynamic tool construction in agent/integration flows, and TLS verification disabled in shared BFF HTTP clients. I’m now checking call chains and false-positive guards for those before promoting anything into submitted findings.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "for f in ab-test-skill-hybrid-v15/work/shards/xspark-tools/shard-summary.json ab-test-skill-hybrid-v15/work/shards/xspark-agent/shard-summary.json ab-test-skill-hybrid-v15/work/shards/xspark-bff/shard-summary.json; do printf '%s\\n' \"## "'$f"; sed -n '"'1,220p' \""'$f"; done'

# Bug Record Schema

Use one Markdown file per Bug. Store structured metadata in the Markdown file itself.

## Contents

- Filename
- Metadata Block
- Required Sections
- Section Substance Bar
- Fix-Ready Sections
- Writing Rules

## Filename

`P<priority>-BUG-<id>-<short-kebab-description>.md`

Examples:

- `P1-BUG-0001-volume-delete-removes-local-record-before-backend-confirm.md`
- `P2-BUG-0042-http-client-has-no-timeout.md`

## Metadata Block

Use a YAML-compatible frontmatter block at the top. Do not create separate YAML files.

```markdown
---
id: BUG-0001
priority: P1
confidence: high
status: open
source: static-analysis
repo: target-service
module: storage
category: data-integrity
issue_family: cross-system-state-mutation
infra_domains: [control-plane, data-integrity, recovery]
fix_risk: medium
entry_points:
  - ResourceService.delete_resource
